planTitle: Wy'East Trailfest 50M Training Plan (v3 — Current block + BPA test through race)

weeks.content:
This is the peak of Build Cycle 2. Big vert week on the long run.

#### Monday - PT Exercises

#### Tuesday - Vert Run + Heavy Strength
**Run**: 8 miles, ~1,600 ft
**Heavy Strength**: Front squats and presses

#### Wednesday - Easy with Hill Strides + PT Exercises
70 min, 8X 20 sec hill strides
- **Distance**: ~6 miles

#### Thursday - Hilly Aerobic + Heavy Strength
80 min hilly
- **Distance**: ~6.5 miles
- **Vert**: ~1,000 ft
**Heavy Strength**: Deadlifts and push presses

#### Friday - Easy + PT Exercises
50 min
- **Distance**: ~4.5 miles

#### Saturday - Day Off

#### Sunday - Long Run
3 hours going for VERT
- **Distance**: ~15 miles
- **Vert**: ~2,500 ft
- **Fueling**: 70-80g/hr

As much vert as you can get in 3 hours. This is a nasty route - BPA, Newton, or similar. Can likely get 2,500-3,000 feet if you're picking right. Effort is going by what feels good. Practice good power hiking and transitioning to running at the top of the hills.

**WEEK 7 COACH COMMENT**:
This is your biggest week yet since returning. Pay attention to how you feel Monday morning. If you're more tired than expected, we might adjust Week 9 after the restore. Good tired = adaptation. Crushing fatigue = too much.

---

days.title: PT Exercises
days.content:
Maintenance — Hip flexors + adductors (injury priority). This is non-negotiable and protecting your future self.

**Circuit (25-30 min):**
- Seated straight leg raise
- SL glute bridge
- Side-lying adduction
- Copenhagen plank (or side plank clam raise)

summary.strengthOneLine: Hip flexors + adductors: SL raise, SL glute bridge, side-lying adduction, Copenhagen plank

days.title: Vert Run + Heavy Strength
days.content:
**Run**: 5 miles, ~1,600 ft

**Heavy Strength - Front squats and presses (foundation)**:

Warm up: Leg swings, goblet squats, banded work, empty bar.

Set 1 - 3–4X through:
- 6X front squats at 65–70%
- 6X strict or push press
- Rest as needed between sets

Set 2 - 2–3X through:
- 8X goblet or front rack reverse lunge per side
- 8X single-arm row per side

**Progression:** Add 5% load or 1 rep when form is solid. By week 4 you should be at 4 rounds on Set 1.

days.title: Easy with Hill Strides + PT Exercises
days.content:
70 min, 8X 20 sec hill strides
- **Distance**: ~3.8 miles

days.title: Hilly Aerobic + Heavy Strength
days.content:
80 min hilly
- **Distance**: ~4.1 miles
- **Vert**: ~1,000 ft

**Heavy Strength - Deadlifts and push presses (hinge + press)**:

Warm up: Good mornings, hip hinges, light deadlifts, shoulder mobility.

Set 1 - 3–4X through:
- 6X deadlifts at 70%
- 6X push press
- Rest as needed

Set 2 - 2–3X through:
- 8X Romanian deadlift or single-leg RDL per side
- 8X single-arm press per side

**Progression:** Build to 4 rounds on Set 1; add weight when 6 reps feel controlled.

weeks.content:
Full week coming off deload. The fitness test is **Wednesday**; Tuesday is easy so you're rested. Rest of the week is normal structure: easy runs, PT, and a moderate long run Sunday.

#### Monday - PT Exercises
Maintenance — hip flexors + adductors. Same circuit as usual.

#### Tuesday - Easy Run + PT Exercises
45 min easy. No heavy strength today; keep legs fresh for the test.
- **Distance**: ~4 miles
- **Effort**: Easy, conversation pace
- **PT**: Wednesday circuit (reactive + knee)

#### Wednesday - FITNESS TEST: 3 BPA repeats
**Test:** 3 BPA repeats. Same route as previous attempts; compare total time and RPE to last time.
**Aim:** 21:30–23:00/min pace if things go well (race-relevant vert and effort).

Warm up easy. Run the test. Cool down. Note your time and how you felt.

#### Thursday - Easy Run + PT (optional light strength)
45–50 min easy. Recovery from the test. Optional light strength if you feel good.
- **Distance**: ~4 miles
- **Purpose**: Blood flow, recovery

#### Friday - Easy Run + PT Exercises
45 min easy + abductors/posterior circuit.
- **Distance**: ~4 miles

#### Saturday - Day Off
Rest. Foam roll if it helps.

#### Sunday - Long Run (moderate)
2–2:15 hours easy to moderate. This keeps the week at full structure and bridges into the next build.
- **Distance**: ~10–11 miles
- **Vert**: ~1,200–1,500 ft (rolling)
- **Effort**: Aerobic, no pushing
- **Fueling**: Practice 60g/hr

**WEEK 4 NOTE:** Test Wednesday; everything else supports recovery and continuity. Use the test result to calibrate the next block.

weeks.content:
We're adding 4X8 min power hike repeats. This is THE workout for Wy'East. You'll be power hiking significant portions of 10,600 feet of gain.

#### Monday - PT Exercises (PROGRESSED)
**Updated circuit (25-30 min):**
- Seated banded hip flexor march: 3x25 + light band ⬆️
- Psoas plank: 3x45s ⬆️
- Side-lying adduction: 3x20
- Copenhagen plank: 3x45s ⬆️
- Monster walks (light band): 3x20ft
- Fonda (light band): 3x20 ⬆️
- SL glute bridge: 4x12 + 15lb dumbbell ⬆️
- Calf raises: 3xAMRAP
- Step-downs: 3x20 slow eccentric ⬆️
- Wall sit: 3x60s ⬆️

#### Tuesday - Vert Run + Heavy Strength
**Run**: 7.5 miles, ~1,700 ft
**Heavy Strength**: Squat power work

#### Wednesday - Hill Strides + PT Exercises
80 min with 8X 20 sec hill strides
- **Distance**: ~6 miles

#### Thursday - 4X8 min Power Hike Repeats + Heavy Strength
Warm up with 15 minutes of flatter running OR if you don't have flat, run/walk and hike the hillier portions.

**Main set: 4X 8 min POWER HIKE UPHILL**
This is the key part of the workout. Try to build your power with each time. NO RUN, all hike, GO. JOG down nice and easy and then head right back up for the next one. Easy jog the flat back to the start.

- **Total time**: ~90 min
- **Distance**: ~5.5 miles
- **Vert**: ~1,500 ft

You will have power hiking parts of your race. I want you FAST when power hiking. The hiking will likely still be in Z2, maybe up to Z3 by the end.

**Heavy Strength**: Deadlifts (lighter day after hike work)

#### Friday - Easy + PT Exercises
50 min
- **Distance**: ~4 miles

#### Saturday - Day Off

#### Sunday - Long Run
4 hours going for vert
- **Distance**: ~17 miles
- **Vert**: ~4,000 ft
- **Fueling**: 80g/hr

As much vert as you can get in 4 hours. Can likely get 4,000+ feet if you're picking your trails right. OK to do repeats. No pace goals, just time on feet and fueling like a champ.

One superpower in ultrarunning is being able to start running right away at the top of a hill going flat or down. This means nailing the effort on the up so you can transition well. That is what I want you to play with today.

Run to your hills, use a few different spots if you want, and do each at least 2X so you can nail the up and over. Aim for a pace as you approach the top that allows for you to run well right away on the way down.

## RACE DAY EXECUTION - WY'EAST 50M

### Fueling Strategy
Aiming for great fueling in the front half so if you have to ease up from heat or GI distress you aren't in a huge deficit.

- Fuel every 20 minutes
- **Minimum**: 60g carbs/hour
- **Optimal**: 75-80g carbs/hour
- Hydrate to thirst but pay attention - less than 1/2L per hour is probably too little
- Never leave an aid station without full water capacity
- If fueling starts feeling gross, switch to all liquids (soda, etc.) - still good calories
- If GI distress hits, check fluid consumption and up it

### Mental Game Tips
- Be your own pacer - ask yourself "What is stopping me from running/faster hiking?"
- When it gets hard, break it into smaller chunks
- Use walk breaks strategically, not as failure
- If you feel yourself spiraling mentally, focus on form cues: "feet, breath, forward lean"

### Pacing
- First 10 miles: CONSERVATIVE. Slower than you think.
- Miles 10-25: Find your rhythm
- Miles 25-40: Execute what you trained
- Miles 40-50: Empty the tank, but smart

### The Walk Break Framework
Walk breaks are STRATEGY, not failure:
- Walk the steep uphills (anything >15% grade)
- Walk when eating/drinking (aids digestion)
- Walk for 30-60 sec every 20-30 min if needed
- Power hike is faster than struggling to run on steep terrain

---

## EMERGENCY PROTOCOLS

### If You Miss a Week
- Skip the missed week entirely
- Do NOT try to "make it up"
- Continue with the current week's plan
- If you miss a restore week, still do an easier week

### If You Feel Overtrained
Signs: meh mood, heavy legs for 3+ days, elevated resting HR, sleep issues

Response:
1. Make current week a restore week
2. Drop intensity first, then volume
3. If running sounds dumb, don't run
4. Contact coach (or check in with yourself honestly)

### If Hip Flexor Flares
- STOP immediately
- 3-5 days complete rest from running
- PT exercises at reduced volume (if pain-free)
- Mobility, foam rolling, gentle movement only
- Restart at 50% of current volume
- Build back slowly

### The "Feel Good, Add More" Trap
When you feel amazing and want to add miles/vert:

**DON'T.**

Write it down. Tell yourself: "I feel great because training is working. If I add more, I break the process that's making me feel great."

The goal is to arrive at Wy'East feeling FRESH and STRONG, not EXHAUSTED and BARELY MAKING IT.

---

## SUMMARY

**Total planned weeks**: 27
**Peak mileage week**: 42 miles (Week 23) — *v2: volume-aligned to 2024 data (31.9 mi avg, 37-38 true training weeks)*
**Peak vert week**: 8,000 ft (Week 23)
**Build pattern**: 3 build, 1 restore (repeat)
**Long run peak**: 5+ hours, 22 miles, 6,000+ ft

**Weekly Structure**:
- Monday: PT Exercises (muscular endurance)
- Tuesday: Vert Run + Heavy Squats
- Wednesday: Easy Run + PT Exercises
- Thursday: Hilly Run + Heavy Deadlifts
- Friday: Easy Run + PT Exercises
- Saturday: OFF
- Sunday: Long Run

Dana got you through 26 weeks injury-free on **~32 mpw average**. This plan (v2) matches that volume:
- Time over mileage
- Vert builds separately from distance
- Walk breaks as strategy
- Heavy strength 2x/week (Tue/Thu)
- PT exercises 3x/week (MWF) - protecting that hip flexor
- Fueling as a trainable skill
- Restore weeks are non-negotiable

Your job: Trust the process, fuel well, and show up on August 15th ready to crush 50 miles.

If race is Saturday Aug 15:
- Monday-Tuesday: Easy runs as above

- Friday: OFF - rest, prep
- **Saturday Aug 15: RACE DAY**

---

## RACE DAY EXECUTION - WY'EAST 50M

### Fueling Strategy
Aiming for great fueling in the front half so if you have to ease up from heat or GI distress you aren't in a huge deficit.

- Fuel every 20 minutes
- **Minimum**: 60g carbs/hour
- **Optimal**: 75-80g carbs/hour
- Hydrate to thirst but pay attention - less than 1/2L per hour is probably too little
- Never leave an aid station without full water capacity
- If fueling starts feeling gross, switch to all liquids (soda, etc.) - still good calories
- If GI distress hits, check fluid consumption and up it

### Mental Game Tips
- Be your own pacer - ask yourself "What is stopping me from running/faster hiking?"
- When it gets hard, break it into smaller chunks
- Use walk breaks strategically, not as failure
- If you feel yourself spiraling mentally, focus on form cues: "feet, breath, forward lean"

### Pacing
- First 10 miles: CONSERVATIVE. Slower than you think.
- Miles 10-25: Find your rhythm
- Miles 25-40: Execute what you trained
- Miles 40-50: Empty the tank, but smart

### The Walk Break Framework
Walk breaks are STRATEGY, not failure:
- Walk the steep uphills (anything >15% grade)
- Walk when eating/drinking (aids digestion)
- Walk for 30-60 sec every 20-30 min if needed
- Power hike is faster than struggling to run on steep terrain

---

## EMERGENCY PROTOCOLS

### If You Miss a Week
- Skip the missed week entirely
- Do NOT try to "make it up"
- Continue with the current week's plan
- If you miss a restore week, still do an easier week

### If You Feel Overtrained
Signs: meh mood, heavy legs for 3+ days, elevated resting HR, sleep issues

Response:
1. Make current week a restore week
2. Drop intensity first, then volume
3. If running sounds dumb, don't run
4. Contact coach (or check in with yourself honestly)

### If Hip Flexor Flares
- STOP immediately
- 3-5 days complete rest from running
- PT exercises at reduced volume (if pain-free)
- Mobility, foam rolling, gentle movement only
- Restart at 50% of current volume
- Build back slowly

### The "Feel Good, Add More" Trap
When you feel amazing and want to add miles/vert:

**DON'T.**

Write it down. Tell yourself: "I feel great because training is working. If I add more, I break the process that's making me feel great."

The goal is to arrive at Wy'East feeling FRESH and STRONG, not EXHAUSTED and BARELY MAKING IT.

---

## SUMMARY

**Total planned weeks**: 27
**Peak mileage week**: 42 miles (Week 23) — *v2: volume-aligned to 2024 data (31.9 mi avg, 37-38 true training weeks)*
**Peak vert week**: 8,000 ft (Week 23)
**Build pattern**: 3 build, 1 restore (repeat)
**Long run peak**: 5+ hours, 22 miles, 6,000+ ft

**Weekly Structure**:
- Monday: PT Exercises (muscular endurance)
- Tuesday: Vert Run + Heavy Squats
- Wednesday: Easy Run + PT Exercises
- Thursday: Hilly Run + Heavy Deadlifts
- Friday: Easy Run + PT Exercises
- Saturday: OFF
- Sunday: Long Run

Dana got you through 26 weeks injury-free on **~32 mpw average**. This plan (v2) matches that volume:
- Time over mileage
- Vert builds separately from distance
- Walk breaks as strategy
- Heavy strength 2x/week (Tue/Thu)
- PT exercises 3x/week (MWF) - protecting that hip flexor
- Fueling as a trainable skill
- Restore weeks are non-negotiable

Your job: Trust the process, fuel well, and show up on August 15th ready to crush 50 miles.

---

*"First 30 minutes are so chill you are the coolest cucumber out there. Then you are going for the mid to top end aerobic pace, so you can sing and chat. Keep focused on great form and fueling. Fuel every 20 minutes and WHEN YOU DO, use that time to take a reset, give yourself a pep talk and keep going."*

— The Dana Method

days.title: PT Exercises
days.content:
Maintenance — Hip flexors + adductors (injury priority). This is non-negotiable and protecting your future self.

**Circuit (25-30 min):**
- Seated straight leg raise
- SL glute bridge
- Side-lying adduction
- Copenhagen plank (or side plank clam raise)
